informal_statement.text: Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is sorted strictly ascending and an integer key, return a new BST identical to the input on every existing key plus an additional node with the given key, unless the key is already present, in which case the tree is returned unchanged.
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order traversal is strictly ascending)
required_properties: the output tree is a valid BST
the multiset of keys in the output equals the multiset of keys in the input union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the multiset of keys in the input
every key present in the input is also present in the output
edge_cases: single-node tree, key less than root
single-node tree, key greater than root
single-node tree, key equal to root (unchanged)

semantic_units.description: BST precondition: the input tree is a valid BST (in-order strictly ascending).
BST invariant preservation: the output tree is also a valid BST.
common_failure_modes: asserting it only when the key is absent

semantic_units.description: Key-set change: the output's key multiset is the input's multiset union {key} if the key was absent, else equals the input's multiset.
common_failure_modes: asserting insertion regardless of duplicates (breaks BST uniqueness)

semantic_units.description: Duplicate no-op: inserting an existing key returns a tree with the same key multiset.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → IsBst (bstInsert t k)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST, the output tree preserves the BST invariant.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → (k ∈ keys t ∨ (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (k :: keys t))
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was absent, the output's keys are the input's keys plus the new key.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (t : Tree) (k : Int), IsBst t → k ∈ keys t → (keys (bstInsert t k)).Perm (keys t)
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree is a valid BST and the key was already present, the output's keys are a permutation of the input's.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
inductive Tree : Type
| Nil : Tree
| Node : Tree → Int → Tree → Tree
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the same immutable binary search tree datatype as in the Rust code (Nil or Node(left, key, right)).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def bstInsert : Tree → Int → Tree
| Tree.Nil, key => Tree.Node Tree.Nil key Tree.Nil
| Tree.Node left k right, key =>
    if key < k then Tree.Node (bstInsert left key) k right
    else if key > k then Tree.Node left k (bstInsert right key)
    else Tree.Node left k right
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the Lean definition of bst insertion matching the Rust pattern match and the three-way comparison (less/greater/no-op on equality).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem bstInsert_terminates (t : Tree) (key : Int) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive definition is structurally recursive on the input tree (so it terminates). (In Lean, this is typically discharged by the termination checker; here we record the obligation as a trivial placeholder.)

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def BST : Tree → Prop
| Tree.Nil => True
| Tree.Node l k r => BST l ∧ BST r ∧ (∀ x, x ∈ keys l → x < k) ∧ (∀ x, x ∈ keys r → k < x)

-- where keys collects all stored keys

def keys : Tree → Set Int
| Tree.Nil => ∅
| Tree.Node l k r => keys l ∪ {k} ∪ keys r

theorem bstInsert_preserves_BST : ∀ t key, BST t → BST (bstInsert t key)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree satisfies the binary search tree ordering invariant, then inserting a key preserves that invariant.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bstInsert_eq (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  (∃ l r, t = Tree.Node l k r) → bstInsert t k = t
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If the tree already contains a node with the same key at the root, insertion is a no-op (matches the `else` branch when `key == k`).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_keys_insert : ∀ t key, key ∈ keys (bstInsert t key) := by
  intro t key
  -- proof by induction on t following the Rust cases
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion, the inserted key is present in the resulting tree.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem mem_keys_other : ∀ t key x,
  x ≠ key → (x ∈ keys t ↔ x ∈ keys (bstInsert t key)) := by
  intro t key x hx
  -- proof by induction on t following the Rust comparisons
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For any key x different from the inserted key, insertion does not remove it; it remains present iff it was present before.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem insert_only_changes_one_branch : ∀ t key,
  match t with
  | Tree.Nil => True
  | Tree.Node l k r =>
      (key < k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node (bstInsert l key) k r) ∧
      (key > k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node l k (bstInsert r key)) ∧
      (key = k → bstInsert t key = Tree.Node l k r)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation only recurses into the left subtree when `key < k`, into the right subtree when `key > k`, and otherwise leaves the node unchanged.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bstInsert_correct : ∀ t key,
  keys (bstInsert t key) = keys t ∪ {key} := by
  intro t key
  -- proof by induction on t using the three-way comparison
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The set of keys in the resulting tree equals the original set plus the inserted key (duplicates do not add new nodes).
